title: Mass. McDonald’s employee to face criminal charges after viral video shows alleged food contamination
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A Southbridge McDonald's employee faces criminal charges after a viral video showed alleged food contamination.
The video showed the employee eating fries and then placing them back into a container for customers.
The Southbridge franchise owners stated involved parties have left, and no public health violations were found.

title: Boston man awaiting trial for allegedly dumping woman’s body found guilty of rape, strangulation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Rinnyers Pena, 47, found guilty of rape, strangulation, and other charges in Boston, Suffolk County DA announced.
Pena acquitted on charges including kidnapping, but faces a separate body disinterment charge linked to Alenny Matos's case.
Prosecutors plan sentencing for Monday, while Pena's attorney expresses optimism about a plea in the remaining case.

title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s 2-1 win over Minnesota United
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution defeated Minnesota United 2-1 at Gillette Stadium, with goals from Carles Gil and Luca Langoni.
Revolution winger Luca Langoni returned, scoring the winning goal after a pressing mistake from Minnesota's Nectarios Triantis.
Goalkeeper Matt Turner made several crucial saves for the Revolution, rebounding after his midweek error against Nashville.

title: SNL recap: Best moments from Will Ferrell hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’ season finale
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Will Ferrell hosted 'Saturday Night Live' season 51 finale on May 16 with Paul McCartney as musical guest.
Ferrell opened the show as Jeffrey Epstein's ghost with a comedic sketch featuring Donald Trump played by James Austin Johnson.
Paul McCartney performed three songs including 'Band on the Run' and joined Ferrell's monologue and a sketch.

title: Flashback: A look at previous times Tall Ships have sailed into Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 2026 will be held from July 11-16, featuring the Sail250 fleet for the U.S. 250th anniversary.
Previous Sail Boston events took place in 1976, 1980, 1992, 2000, 2009, 2012, and 2017, drawing significant crowds.
The 1992 event faced protests over historical associations with slavery, but high attendance continued in subsequent Sail Boston events.

title: Vibe in Patriots building reportedly better than two weeks ago with Mike Vrabel
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Mike Vrabel returns to Patriots rookie minicamp with renewed energy despite offseason controversies.
Patriots' acquisition strategy includes key signings and potential trade for WR A.J. Brown post-June 1.
The Patriots face a challenging 2026 schedule with games against top teams like the Chiefs and Bills.

title: Willson Contreras’s go-ahead HR, Payton Tolle’s 8 strong innings lift Red Sox past Braves 3-2
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a decisive two-run homer in the eighth inning, lifting the Red Sox to a 3-2 victory.
Payton Tolle pitched a career-high eight innings, allowing two runs, and facilitated Boston's comeback against the Braves.
Aroldis Chapman secured the save with a bases-loaded situation, ensuring the Red Sox's victory against MLB's top-ranked team.

title: Red Sox place Trevor Story on injured list with sports hernia, contemplating surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Trevor Story placed on 10-day injured list with a sports hernia, decision on surgery pending.
Nick Sogard recalled from Triple-A Worcester to replace Story ahead of Braves game.
Story has career-low .206 batting average and has committed six errors this season.

title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 74-year-old driver died after his pickup truck collided with a school bus in Marlborough, NH.
The school bus was carrying 13 students; none of the students sustained serious injuries.
The collision is under investigation by NH State Police, focusing on why the pickup entered the wrong lane.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski's walk-off hit in the 10th inning led the Braves to a 3-2 victory over the Red Sox.
Red Sox starter Connelly Early allowed two solo home runs and struck out six over five innings in the loss.
Boston's Marcelo Mayer hit a solo home run to tie the game, but the Red Sox struggled with runners in scoring position.
